goal: Produce an auditable maximum-allowable-transient-contact-force calculation for UR10e wrist impact on body region 15 (forehead) per ISO/TS 15066 Annex A, with every input traced to the standard.
thoughts: Never quote ISO numbers without verification. Region-15 transient/quasi-static limits must come from lookup_knowledge hitting the actual ISO/TS 15066 Annex A.2 table, not from memory. UR10e reduced-mode flange mass must come from lookup_product_spec against UR documentation. check_physics_health only confirms the sim substep config before any cross-check — sim never defines the limit.
code:
# The standard defines the limit. Sim checks whether the robot exceeds it.

# 1. Pull the ACTUAL biomechanical table — body region 15 (forehead/skull)
lookup_knowledge(query="ISO TS 15066 Annex A Table A.2 body region 15 forehead transient quasi-static limit")

# 2. Pull UR10e tool-flange mass + reduced-mode inertia from vendor docs
lookup_product_spec(product="Universal Robots UR10e", field="tool_flange_mass_reduced_mode")

# 3. Confirm the sim's contact-solver substep config BEFORE any cross-check
check_physics_health(stage_path="<COBOT_CELL_USD>")

# What to produce for the hazard log — a table, not a single number:
#   Input              | Value        | Source
#   -----------------  | -----------  | ------------------------------
#   Body region        | 15 (forehead)| Task statement
#   Transient limit    | <FROM_ISO>   | ISO/TS 15066 Annex A.2 row 15
#   Quasi-static limit | <FROM_ISO>   | ISO/TS 15066 Annex A.2 row 15
#   Spring constant    | <FROM_ISO>   | ISO/TS 15066 Annex A.3
#   Effective mass     | <FROM_UR>    | UR10e datasheet + payload
#   Contact geometry   | r=10 mm tip  | Given
# If lookup_knowledge returns no confirmed row 15 values, STOP and tell Thomas
# the retrieval failed — do NOT paste numbers from memory.
failure_modes: Conflating transient (higher) and quasi-static (lower) limits into one 'force limit'
Rounding region-specific limits to a whole-body average — explicitly forbidden by the standard
Using the PhysX-reported peak force as the limit instead of comparing it against the ISO limit
Fabricating ISO numbers when lookup_knowledge fails to resolve the table
